mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects expanding labor income that drives personal consumption and corporate revenue; this momentum transmits into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2013 nonfarm payrolls at 136038.0 thousand persons
failure_condition: Correlation between month-over-month PAYEMS change and 20-day forward S&P 500 return is negative over a 3-year rolling window, or the mean return following positive PAYEMS growth is not statistically above zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.5 percent, the Federal Reserve's forward guidance anchors expectations for prolonged zero interest rates, lowering the equity discount rate and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods with UNRATE > 6.5%, the average 60-day S&P 500 forward return is negative or the hit rate of positive returns is below 50% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near zero while consumer price inflation accelerates, the resulting negative real policy rate erodes the real return on safe assets and drives capital reallocation into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2013 Fed funds at 0.09% and CPIAUCSL at 233.318
failure_condition: Joint realizations of the trigger condition fail to produce positive average 20-day returns (t-test p > 0.1) or the interaction term in a bivariate regression does not carry a positive coefficient significant at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: During post-crisis recovery, rising housing starts (HOUST) signal a rebound in residential construction, durable goods demand, and household net worth, which lifts cyclical earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2013 housing starts at 836.0 thousand units
failure_condition: During months classified as post-crisis recovery, a one-standard-deviation increase in HOUST is followed by negative average 5-day S&P 500 returns over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) indicates rising capacity utilization and operating leverage for manufacturing firms, translating into higher earnings surprises and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year correlation between month-over-month INDPRO growth and 20-day S&P 500 forward returns falls below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) expanding at a pace above 2.0 percent annualized signals excess aggregate demand that boosts corporate top-line growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Q2 2013 real GDP level at 15648.7B chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.0% annualized are followed by negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns over a rolling 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Nominal retail sales (RSAFS) growth above 0.5 percent month-over-month signals robust consumer spending momentum that outpaces inventory accumulation, lifting revenue growth for consumer-discretionary and industrial firms and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2013 retail sales at 424481.0 million dollars
failure_condition: Months with RSAFS growth > 0.5% fail to produce positive average 5-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 0.5 percent signals falling capacity utilization, inventory overhang, and margin compression for cyclical firms, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with INDPRO contracting more than 0.5% are followed by positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling window.
